bins.name: LCEP Service Proposal
bins.desc: Constellation proposes Load-Contingent New Capacity at Existing Plants (LCEP) as a new interconnection service that pairs new generation and load at existing generator sites. Because the new combination falls within already-studied thermal power flow parameters, it can be processed on an expedited basis outside the standard queue, similar to Surplus Interconnection Service. Constellation asks FERC to include LCEP in the ANOPR replacement rate.
quotes: Constellation submits these supplemental comments to outline a new, reliable, speed-to-service option pairing new generation with large loads at existing power plant sites of at least the same size, and to urge this option's inclusion in the Commission's next steps on the Advanced Notice of Proposed Rulemaking.

This proposal achieves Secretary Wright's objective "to ensure efficient, timely, and non-discriminatory [large] load interconnections" by adding paired new generation and load without changing injection rights or creating withdrawal rights at existing generation sites.

LCEP Service as outlined here and in Attachment A is just and reasonable and not unduly discriminatory or preferential. Like Surplus Interconnection Service, LCEP Service is an expedited interconnection process outside of the standard interconnection queue for new generation paired with new load at sites with existing generation facilities that have already been studied.
The LCEP Proposal is animated by and can achieve the goal of speed to power for large load projects stymied today by extremely long interconnection timelines.
All such study processes must be tailored to accommodate speed to service or the efficiencies of siting at Points of Interconnection already proven to be reliable will be lost.
LCEP Service is one of the simplest and most efficient ways to achieve speed-to-service without jeopardizing reliability or increasing costs to other customers.

bins.name: Existing LGIP Unjust and Unreasonable
bins.desc: Constellation argues FERC must make the FPA section 206 finding that the current pro forma LGIP is unjust and unreasonable because it fails to accommodate paired generator/load combinations at existing, already-studied POIs. Not using proven infrastructure while new load waits is itself an unjust outcome.
quotes: Any final rule on the ANOPR must find that existing tariffs are unjust and unreasonable because they fail to meet "unprecedented current and expected growth of large loads seeking to interconnect to the transmission system" and find that new "standardize[d] interconnection procedures and agreements for such loads, including those seeking to share a point of interconnection with new or existing generation facilities (hybrid facilities)" are required.
It is unjust and unreasonable not to fully utilize existing infrastructure and proven reliability paths while new load waits for service.

Where additional load, paired with new generation, can be served at an existing Point of Interconnection within the already studied parameters, it is unjust and unreasonable that the existing pro forma LGIP does not provide a pathway to do so.

bins.name: LCEP Operational Constraints
bins.desc: Under LCEP, new generation must be sized to match the new load and may not inject net power onto the grid beyond the existing generator's rights. Mechanisms similar to surplus or hybrid interconnection would apply when load reduces or drops, ensuring the no-net-injection condition is always maintained.
quotes: The new generation must be "commensurate with the new load" and "deliverable" only to the new load with which it is paired—no net injections onto the grid beyond the existing generator's rights would be allowed.

bins.name: Transparent Study Requirements
bins.desc: Constellation asks FERC to require transmission providers to clearly define the studies applicable to LCEP requests. Without clear and transparent study rules, the speed benefit of siting at an already-studied POI will be lost to procedural delays.
quotes: The Commission should also require Transmission Providers to clearly identify the studies for evaluating LCEP Service requests. Clear and transparent rules on the studies performed by Transmission Providers are critical to prevent delay.

bins.name: FERC Jurisdiction Over LCEP
bins.desc: Constellation contends FERC has clear authority over LCEP arrangements because the load connects at a FERC-jurisdictional POI, making the load interconnection itself a jurisdictional transmission service.
quotes: Because the existing generator's Point of Interconnection is FERC-jurisdictional, so too must be the load's.

bins.name: FERC Authority Over Load Interconnection
bins.desc: Constellation supports FERC jurisdiction over large load interconnections at transmission-level POIs, arguing that jurisdiction over an existing generator's POI necessarily extends to load connected there.
quotes: Any final rule on the ANOPR must find that existing tariffs are unjust and unreasonable because they fail to meet "unprecedented current and expected growth of large loads seeking to interconnect to the transmission system" and find that new "standardize[d] interconnection procedures and agreements for such loads, including those seeking to share a point of interconnection with new or existing generation facilities (hybrid facilities)" are required.
It is unjust and unreasonable not to fully utilize existing infrastructure and proven reliability paths while new load waits for service.
Because the existing generator's Point of Interconnection is FERC-jurisdictional, so too must be the load's.

bins.name: Hybrid Generator-Load Rights
bins.desc: Constellation argues current rules fail to define rights for paired generator/load combinations at existing POIs. LCEP fills this gap by giving new generation rights that are contingent on the paired load being online and capped at the existing generator's injection rights.
quotes: Constellation's analysis identified one particular lost opportunity: the current rules do not clearly accommodate the pairing of new generator/load combinations at existing generator's Points of Interconnection that create no new thermal power flow conditions which have not already been studied.

Where additional load, paired with new generation, can be served at an existing Point of Interconnection within the already studied parameters, it is unjust and unreasonable that the existing pro forma LGIP does not provide a pathway to do so.

The new generation must be "commensurate with the new load" and "deliverable" only to the new load with which it is paired—no net injections onto the grid beyond the existing generator's rights would be allowed.

bins.name: Joint Study of Load and Generation
bins.desc: Constellation supports studying load and generation together under ANOPR Principle 3. The LCEP proposal operationalizes joint study by building on already-completed studies at existing POIs, reducing the need for new network upgrade analyses.
quotes: All such study processes must be tailored to accommodate speed to service or the efficiencies of siting at Points of Interconnection already proven to be reliable will be lost.

The Commission should also require Transmission Providers to clearly identify the studies for evaluating LCEP Service requests. Clear and transparent rules on the studies performed by Transmission Providers are critical to prevent delay.

LCEP Service also is fully responsive to the ANOPR's directives and principles, including to study load together with Generating Facilities, given "siting a large load near or at the same point of interconnection as a new generating facility could reduce the network upgrades needed to interconnect only the load or only the generating facility" (Third Principle), and incentivizing co-location which can "ensure[] efficient buildout of the transmission system" (Fifth Principle).

bins.name: Expedited Queue Treatment
bins.desc: Constellation asks FERC to allow LCEP requests to be processed outside the standard interconnection queue on an expedited basis, similar to Surplus Interconnection Service, to address years-long delays facing large loads.
quotes: LCEP Service as outlined here and in Attachment A is just and reasonable and not unduly discriminatory or preferential. Like Surplus Interconnection Service, LCEP Service is an expedited interconnection process outside of the standard interconnection queue for new generation paired with new load at sites with existing generation facilities that have already been studied.
The LCEP Proposal is animated by and can achieve the goal of speed to power for large load projects stymied today by extremely long interconnection timelines.

bins.name: Streamlined Study Process
bins.desc: Constellation supports reforming interconnection study processes to build on existing studies at existing POIs. LCEP would confirm that already-studied thermal conditions remain valid rather than conducting full new studies, reducing time and cost.
quotes: Constellation submits these supplemental comments to outline a new, reliable, speed-to-service option pairing new generation with large loads at existing power plant sites of at least the same size, and to urge this option's inclusion in the Commission's next steps on the Advanced Notice of Proposed Rulemaking.

This proposal achieves Secretary Wright's objective "to ensure efficient, timely, and non-discriminatory [large] load interconnections" by adding paired new generation and load without changing injection rights or creating withdrawal rights at existing generation sites.

Any final rule on the ANOPR must find that existing tariffs are unjust and unreasonable because they fail to meet "unprecedented current and expected growth of large loads seeking to interconnect to the transmission system" and find that new "standardize[d] interconnection procedures and agreements for such loads, including those seeking to share a point of interconnection with new or existing generation facilities (hybrid facilities)" are required.

LCEP Service as outlined here and in Attachment A is just and reasonable and not unduly discriminatory or preferential. Like Surplus Interconnection Service, LCEP Service is an expedited interconnection process outside of the standard interconnection queue for new generation paired with new load at sites with existing generation facilities that have already been studied.
The LCEP Proposal is animated by and can achieve the goal of speed to power for large load projects stymied today by extremely long interconnection timelines.
All such study processes must be tailored to accommodate speed to service or the efficiencies of siting at Points of Interconnection already proven to be reliable will be lost.

The Commission should also require Transmission Providers to clearly identify the studies for evaluating LCEP Service requests. Clear and transparent rules on the studies performed by Transmission Providers are critical to prevent delay.
LCEP Service is one of the simplest and most efficient ways to achieve speed-to-service without jeopardizing reliability or increasing costs to other customers.

LCEP Service also is fully responsive to the ANOPR's directives and principles, including to study load together with Generating Facilities, given "siting a large load near or at the same point of interconnection as a new generating facility could reduce the network upgrades needed to interconnect only the load or only the generating facility" (Third Principle), and incentivizing co-location which can "ensure[] efficient buildout of the transmission system" (Fifth Principle).

bins.name: Co-location at Existing Plants
bins.desc: Constellation supports co-location of generation and load as an ANOPR principle and proposes LCEP as the specific mechanism. Siting new generation and load at an existing plant's POI reduces network upgrade needs and speeds interconnection.
quotes: Constellation submits these supplemental comments to outline a new, reliable, speed-to-service option pairing new generation with large loads at existing power plant sites of at least the same size, and to urge this option's inclusion in the Commission's next steps on the Advanced Notice of Proposed Rulemaking.

This proposal achieves Secretary Wright's objective "to ensure efficient, timely, and non-discriminatory [large] load interconnections" by adding paired new generation and load without changing injection rights or creating withdrawal rights at existing generation sites.

LCEP Service also is fully responsive to the ANOPR's directives and principles, including to study load together with Generating Facilities, given "siting a large load near or at the same point of interconnection as a new generating facility could reduce the network upgrades needed to interconnect only the load or only the generating facility" (Third Principle), and incentivizing co-location which can "ensure[] efficient buildout of the transmission system" (Fifth Principle).

bins.name: Flexible Load-Contingent Operations
bins.desc: Constellation proposes operational flexibility mechanisms for LCEP to handle load reduction or outage events, mirroring surplus and hybrid interconnection protections, so that no-net-injection conditions are always met.
quotes: The new generation must be "commensurate with the new load" and "deliverable" only to the new load with which it is paired—no net injections onto the grid beyond the existing generator's rights would be allowed.

bins.name: Using Proven Proximate Infrastructure
bins.desc: Constellation argues that connecting load at sites with existing, proven reliability paths is the most efficient use of the grid and that leaving such capacity idle while load waits is unjust.
quotes: It is unjust and unreasonable not to fully utilize existing infrastructure and proven reliability paths while new load waits for service.

bins.name: No Cost Shift to Existing Customers
bins.desc: Constellation argues LCEP achieves speed-to-service without imposing new costs on other customers because it uses existing studied infrastructure rather than triggering new network upgrades.
quotes: LCEP Service is one of the simplest and most efficient ways to achieve speed-to-service without jeopardizing reliability or increasing costs to other customers.
